description:
You are acting as a database administrator who is tidying up some query-tuning scripts.  
A folder already exists that contains those scripts:

• /home/user/db_scripts/schema.sql  
• /home/user/db_scripts/optimize_queries.sql  

Your goal is to archive that directory and register the action in a log, all from the command line in ONE single compound command (i.e. everything is typed on one line and then executed once).

Required end-state

1. A compressed archive must exist at  
   /home/user/backups/db_scripts_backup_20231015.tar.gz  
   The archive must unpack to a top-level directory called db_scripts/ which, in turn, must contain the two SQL files listed above.  
   No other files or directories may be present inside the tarball.

2. A log file must exist at  
   /home/user/backup_logs/backup_status.log  
   The file must contain exactly one line (plus a trailing newline) and that line must conform **precisely** to this fixed format:  

   2023-10-15 10:00:00 | db_scripts_backup_20231015.tar.gz | SUCCESS  

   Notes about the format:  
   • The timestamp string is literal; do NOT substitute the current date/time.  
   • Fields are separated by a single space, a vertical bar (“|”), and another single space.  
   • The line must end with a single newline character (Unix line ending).

3. Both the archive creation and the log-file entry must be produced by running a single terminal command line (you may chain sub-commands with “&&” or “;”, but everything must be executed after one press of <Enter>).  

4. Do not use sudo or any privileged operation; everything must be created with the default permissions of the current user.

The automated grader will unpack the tarball and inspect /home/user/backup_logs/backup_status.log for the exact contents specified above, so pay close attention to names, paths, and the log-line format.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent begins:

Directory: /home/user/db_scripts  
  ├── schema.sql  
  │     Content: "-- Schema definition placeholder\n"  
  └── optimize_queries.sql  
        Content: "-- Query optimization placeholder\n"

Directory: /home/user/backups            (exists, empty)
Directory: /home/user/backup_logs        (exists, empty)

Expected final filesystem state after successful completion:

1. /home/user/backups/db_scripts_backup_20231015.tar.gz  
   • When extracted, the tarball produces one top-level directory named db_scripts/ containing exactly:  
       - schema.sql      (content unchanged)  
       - optimize_queries.sql (content unchanged)

2. /home/user/backup_logs/backup_status.log  
   • Exact file contents (including final newline):

     2023-10-15 10:00:00 | db_scripts_backup_20231015.tar.gz | SUCCESS\n

No additional files, directories, or log entries should exist.
